items.arabic: رَضيـتُ بِاللهِ رَبَّـاً وَبِالإسْلامِ ديـناً وَبِمُحَـمَّدٍ نَبِيّـاً . (ثلاث مرات)
items.transliteration: Raḍītu billāhi Rabba, wa bil-Islāmi dīna, wa bi-Muḥammadin (ṣallallāhu `alayhi wa sallama) nabiyya.
items.english: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islam as my religion, and with Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) as my Prophet. (Recite three times in Arabic.)
items.reference: 'Allah has promised that anyone who says this three times every morning or evening will be pleased on the Day of Resurrection.' Ahmad 4/ 337, An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah p. 4, Ibn As-Sunni (no. 68), At-Tirmidhi 5/465. Its chain of transmission is good (Hasan), Ibn Baz, p. 39. Hisn al-Muslim 87

items.arabic: يا حَـيُّ يا قَيّـومُ بِـرَحْمَـتِكِ أَسْتَـغـيث ، أَصْلِـحْ لي شَـأْنـي كُلَّـه ، وَلا تَكِلـني إِلى نَفْـسي طَـرْفَةَ عَـين
items.transliteration: Yā Ḥayyu yā Qayyūmu biraḥmatika astaghīth aṣlih lī sha'nī kullah wa lā takilnī ilā nafsī ṭarfata `ayn.
items.english: O Ever-Living One, O Eternal One, by Your mercy I call on You to set right all my affairs. Do not place me in charge of my soul even for the blinking of an eye (i.e. a moment).
items.reference: Its chain of transmission is sound (Sahih), Al-Hakim 1/545, see Albani, Sahihut-Targhib wat-Tarhib, 1/273. Hisn al-Muslim 88

items.arabic: أَصْبَـحْـنا وَأَصْبَـحْ المُـلكُ للهِ رَبِّ العـالَمـين ، اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أسْـأَلُـكَ خَـيْرَ هـذا الـيَوْم ، فَـتْحَهُ ، وَنَصْـرَهُ ، وَنـورَهُ وَبَـرَكَتَـهُ ، وَهُـداهُ ، وَأَعـوذُ بِـكَ مِـنْ شَـرِّ ما فـيهِ وَشَـرِّ ما بَعْـدَه  وإذا أمسى قال: أَمْسَيْـنا وَأَمْسـى المُـلكُ للهِ رَبِّ العـالَمـين ، اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أسْـأَلُـكَ خَـيْرَ هـذهِ اللَّـيْلَة ، فَتْحَهـا ، وَنَصْـرَهـا ، وَنـورَهـا وَبَـرَكَتَـهـا ، وَهُـداهـا ، وَأَعـوذُ بِـكَ مِـنْ شَـرِّ ما فـيهـاِ وَشَـرِّ ما بَعْـدَهـا
items.transliteration: Aṣbaḥnā wa aṣbaḥal-mulku lillāhi Rabbi ‘l-a`lāmīn, Allāhumma innī as'aluka khayra hādha ‘l-yawm: Fat’ḥahu wa naṣrahu wa nūrahu, wa barakatahu, wa hudāh, wa a`ūdhu bika min sharri mā fīhi wa sharri mā ba`dah.
items.english: We have entered a new day and with it all the dominion which belongs to Allah, Lord of all that exists. O Allah, I ask You for the goodness of this day,2 its victory, its help, its light, its blessings, and its guidance. I seek refuge in You from the evil that is in it, and from the evil that follows it.
items.reference: 1 For evening recitation, say here: Allāhumma innī as'aluka khayra hāthihil-laylah: 'My Lord, I ask You for the good things of this night.' 2 Abu Dawud 4/322. Its transmission chain is good (Hasan). See also Ibn Al-Qayyim, Zadul- Ma'ad 2/273. Hisn al-Muslim 89

items.arabic: أَصْـبَحْنا[أَمْسَـينا] علـى فِطْـرَةِ الإسْلام، وَعَلـى كَلِـمَةِ الإخْـلاص، وَعلـى دينِ نَبِـيِّنا مُحَـمَّدٍ وَعَلـى مِلَّـةِ أبينـا إِبْـراهيـمَ حَنيـفاً مُسْلِـماً وَمـا كـانَ مِنَ المُشـرِكيـن
items.transliteration: Aṣbahnā `alā fiṭrati ‘l-Islām, wa `alā kalimati ‘l-ikhlās, wa `alā dīni nabiyyinā Muḥammadin (ṣallallāhu `alayhi wa sallam), wa `alā millati abīnā Ibrāhīm, ḥanīfan Musliman wa mā kāna mina ‘l-mushrikīn.
items.english: We have entered a new day 1 upon the natural religion of Islam, the word of sincere devotion, the religion of our Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), and the faith of our father Ibrahim. He was upright (in worshipping Allah), and a Muslim. He was not of those who worship others besides Allah. 2
items.reference: 1 When you say this in the evening, you should say: 'Amsaynā `alā fiṭratil-Islām...: 'We end this day...' 2 Ahmad 3/406-7, 5/123, An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul- Yawm wal-Laylah (no. 34), At-Tirmidhi 4/209. Hisn al-Muslim 90

items.arabic: سُبْحـانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْـدِهِ . (مائة مرة)
items.transliteration: Subḥānallāhi wa biḥamdih.
items.english: Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him. (Recite one hundred times in Arabic).
items.reference: 'Whoever recites this one hundred times in the morning and in the evening will not be surpassed on the Day of Resurrection by anyone having done better than this except for someone who had recited it more. ' Al-Bukhari 4/2071. Hisn al-Muslim 91

items.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وحْـدَهُ لا شَـريكَ لهُ، لهُ المُـلْكُ ولهُ الحَمْـد، وهُوَ على كُلّ شَيءٍ قَدير . (عشر مرات أَوْ مرَّةً واحدةً عندَ الكَسَلِ)
items.transliteration: Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu ‘l-mulku walahu ‘l-ḥamd, wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in qadīr.
items.english: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise and He is Able to do all things. (Recite ten times 1 in Arabic or one time to ward off laziness.) 2
items.reference: 1Allah will write ten Hasanaat (rewards) for whoever recites this ten times in the morning, and forgive him ten misdeeds and give him the reward of freeing ten slaves and protect him from Satan. Whoever recites this ten times in the evening will get this same reward. An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah (no. 24). Its chain of transmission is sound (Sahih). Albani 1/272. Abu Hurayrah «fe narrated that the Prophet j§ said: 'Allah will write one hundred Hasanat for whoever says There is no God but Allah alone, He has no partner. To Allah is possession of everything, and to Him all praise is. He is Capable of all things' ten times in the morning, and forgive him one hundred misdeeds. He will have the reward of freeing a slave and will be protected from Satan throughout the day unto dusk. Whoever says it in the evening will have the same reward.' Ahmad 8/704, 16/293. Its chain of transmission is good (Hasan), Ibn Baz, p. 44. 2 Whoever recites this in the morning, will have the reward of freeing a slave from the Children of Isma'il. Ten Hasanaat (rewards) will be written for him, and he will be forgiven ten misdeeds, raised up ten degrees, and be protected from Satan until evening. Whoever says it in the evening will have the same reward until morning. Abu Dawud 4/319, 3/957, Ahmad 4/ 60, Ibn Majah 2/331, Ibn Al-Qayyim Zadul-Ma'ad 2/388. Its chain of transmission is sound (Sahih). Al-Albani 1/270. Hisn al-Muslim 92

items.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وحْـدَهُ لا شَـريكَ لهُ، لهُ المُـلْكُ ولهُ الحَمْـد، وهُوَ على كُلّ شَيءٍ قَدير . (مائة مرة إذا أصبح)
items.transliteration: Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu ‘l-mulku walahu ‘l-ḥamd, wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in qadīr.
items.english: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise and He is Able to do all things. (Recite 100 times in Arabic upon rising in the morning).
items.reference: Whoever recites this one hundred times a day will have the reward of freeing ten slaves. One hundred Hasanaat (rewards) will be written for him and one hundred misdeeds will be washed away. He will be shielded from Satan until the evening. No one will be able to present anything better than this except for someone who has recited more than this. Al-Bukhari 4/95, Muslim 4/2071.

items.arabic: سُبْحـانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْـدِهِ عَدَدَ خَلْـقِه ، وَرِضـا نَفْسِـه ، وَزِنَـةَ عَـرْشِـه ، وَمِـدادَ كَلِمـاتِـه . (ثلاث مرات إذا أصبح)
items.transliteration: Subḥānallāhi wa biḥamdih: `adada khalqih, wa riḍā nafsih, wa zinata `arshih, wa midāda kalimātih.
items.english: Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him, by the multitude of His creation, by His Pleasure, by the weight of His Throne, and by the extent of His Words. (Recite three times in Arabic upon rising in the morning.)
items.reference: Muslim 4/2090. Hisn al-Muslim 94

items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْماً نَافِعاً، وَرِزْقاً طَيِّباً، وَعَمَلاً مُتَقَبَّلاً (إذا أصبحَ)
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innī as'aluka `ilman nāfi`a, wa rizqan ṭayyiba, wa `amalan mutaqabbala.
items.english: O Allah, I ask You for knowledge that is of benefit, a good provision, and deeds that will be accepted. (Recite in Arabic upon rising in the morning.)
items.reference: Ibn As-Sunni, no. 54, Ibn Majah no. 925. Its chain of transmission is good (Hasan), Ibn Al-Qayyim 2/375. Hisn al-Muslim 95

items.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ (مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ فِي الْيَوْمِ)
items.transliteration: Astaghfirullāha wa atūbu ilayh.
items.english: I seek the forgiveness of Allah and repent to Him. (Recite one hundred times in Arabic during the day.)
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/101, Muslim 4/2075. Hisn al-Muslim 96

items.arabic: أَعـوذُ بِكَلِمـاتِ اللّهِ التّـامّـاتِ مِنْ شَـرِّ ما خَلَـق (ثلاث مرات إِذا أمسى)
items.transliteration: A`ūdhu bikalimāti-llāhit-tāmmāti min sharri mā khalaq.
items.english: I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah from the evil of what He has created. (Recite three times in Arabic in the evening.)
items.reference: Whoever recites this three times in the evening will be protected from insect stings, Ahmad 2/ 290, An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah no. 590, At-Tirmidhi 3/187, Ibn As-Sunni no. 68. According to Al-Albani, Ibn Majah's (2/266) chain of transmission is sound (Sahih), and following Ibn Baz 45, At-Tirmidhi's report is good (Hasan). Hisn al-Muslim 97

items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ وَ سَلِّمْ عَلَى نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ (عشر مرات)
items.transliteration: Allāhumma ṣalli wa sallim `alā nabiyyinā Muḥammad.
items.english: O Allah, we ask for your peace and blessings upon our Prophet Muhammad. (Recite ten times in Arabic.)
items.reference: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Who recites blessings upon me ten times in the morning and ten times in the evening will obtain my intercession on the Day of Resurrection.' At-Tabarani reported this Hadith together with two chains of transmission. One of them is reliable (Jayyid). See Haythami's Majma'uz-Zawa'id 10/120, and Al-Albani's Sahihut-Targhib wat-Tarhib 1/273. Hisn al-Muslim 98

items.arabic: يَجْمَعُ كَفَّيْهِ ثُمَّ يَنْفُثُ فِيهِمَا فَيَقْرَأُ فِيهِمَا: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ {قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ * اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ* لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ* وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُواً أَحَدٌ}. بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ {قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ* مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ* وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ* وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ* وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ}. بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ {قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ* مَلِكِ النَّاسِ* إِلَهِ النَّاسِ* مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ* الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ* مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ} ثُمَّ يَمْسَحُ بِهِمَا مَا اسْتَطَاعَ مِنْ جَسَدِهِ يَبْدَأُ بِهِمَا عَلَى رَأْسِهِ وَوَجْهِهِ وَمَا أَقبَلَ مِنْ جَسَدِهِ (يفعلُ ذلك ثلاثَ مرَّاتٍ)
items.transliteration: Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥīm. Qul huwallāhu aḥad. Allāhuṣ-ṣamad. Lam yalid wa lam yūlad. Wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan aḥad. Reference: Al-Ikhlas 112:1-4. Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥīm. Qul a`ūdhu birabbil-falaq. Min sharri mā khalaq. Wa min sharri ghāsiqin idhā waqab. Wa min sharrin-naffāthāti fil-`uqad. Wa min sharri ḥāsidin idhā ḥasad. Reference: Al-Falaq 113:1-5. Bismillāhir-Raḥmānir-Raḥīm. Qul a`ūdhu birabbin-nās. Malikin-nās. 'Ilāhin-nās. Min sharri ‘l-waswāsil-khannās. Alladhī yuwaswisu fī ṣudūrin-nās. Minal-jinnati wannās. Reference: An-Nas 114:1-6
items.english: Hold the palms together, blow (with a little spittle) into them, and recite: 'With the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say: He is Allah (the) One. The Self-Sufficient Master, Whom all creatures need, He begets not nor was He begotten, and there is none equal to Him. With the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say: I seek refuge with (Allah) the Lord of the daybreak, from the evil of what He has created, and from the evil of the darkening (night) as it comes with its darkness, and from the evil of those who practice witchcraft when they blow in the knots, and from the evil of the envier when he envies. With the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Say: I seek refuge with (Allah) the Lord of mankind, the King of mankind, the God of mankind, from the evil of the whisperer who withdraws, who whispers in the breasts of mankind, of jinns and men.' (Then pass your hands over as much of your body as you can reach, beginning with the head and the face, then the entire front of your body. Do this three times.)
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 9/62, and Muslim 4/ 1723. Hisn al-Muslim 99

items.arabic: ((اللَّهُ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ لاَ تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلاَ نَوْمٌ لَّهُ مَا فِي السَّمَوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الأَرْضِ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِنْدَهُ إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِهِ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَلاَ يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلاَّ بِمَا شَاء وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالأَرْضَ وَلاَ يَؤُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ))
items.transliteration: Allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa ‘l-Ḥayyul-Qayyūm, lā ta'khudhuhu sinatun wa lā nawm, lahu mā fis-samāwāti wa māfil-ardh, man dhal-ladhī yashfa`u `indahu illā bi'idhnih, ya`lamu mā bayna aydīhim wa mā khalfahum, wa lā yuḥītūna bishay'im-min `ilmihi illā bimā shā', wasi`a kursiyyuhus-samāwāti wal-ardh, wa lā ya'ūduhu hifẓuhumā, wa huwal-`Aliyyu‘l-`Aẓīm.
items.english: Allah! There is none worthy of worship but He, the Ever-Living, the One Who sustains and protects all that exists. Neither slumber nor sleep overtakes Him. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is he that can intercede with Him except with His Permission? He knows what happens to them in this world, and what will happen to them in the Hereafter. And they will never encompass anything of His Knowledge except that which He wills. His Throne extends over the heavens and the earth, and He feels no fatigue in guarding and preserving them. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
items.reference: Al-Baqarah 2:255. Whoever reads this when he lies down to sleep will have a guardian from Allah remain with him and Satan will not be able to come near him until he rises in the morning. See Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 4/487. Hisn al-Muslim 100

items.arabic: ((ءامَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ ءامَنَ بِاللهِ وَمَلآئِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لاَ نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّن رُّسُلِهِ وَقَالُواْ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ {285} لاَ يُكَلِّفُ اللهُ نَفْسًا إِلاَّ وُسْعَهَا لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ رَبَّنَا لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِن نَّسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا رَبَّنَا وَلاَ تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا رَبَّنَا وَلاَ تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لاَ طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَآ أَنتَ مَوْلاَنَا فَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ {286}))
items.transliteration: Āmanar-Rasūlu bimā unzila ilaihi mir-Rabbihi wa ‘l-mu'minūn, kullun āmana billāhi wa malā'ikatihi wa kutubihi wa rusulih, lā nufarriqu bayna aḥadim-mir-rusulih, wa qālū sami`nā wa aṭa`nā, ghufrānaka Rabbanā wa ilayka ‘l-maṣīr. Lā yukallifu ‘llāhu nafsan illā wus`ahā, lahā mā kasabat wa `alayhā mak-tasabat, Rabbanā lā tu'ākhidhnā in nasīnā aw akhta'nā, Rabbanā wa lā taḥmil `alaynā iṣran kamā ḥamaltahu `alal-ladhīna min qablinā, Rabbanā wa lā tuḥammilnā mā lā ṭāqata lanā bih, wa`fu `annā, waghfir lanā, warḥamnā, Anta mawlānā fanṣurnā `ala ‘l-qawmi ‘l-kāfirīn.
items.english: The Messenger believes in what has been sent down to him from his Lord, and so do the believers. Each one believes in Allah, His Angels, His Books, and His Messengers. They say: 'We make no distinction between any of His Messengers,' and they say: 'We hear, and we obey. (We seek) Your Forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the return.' Allah burdens not a person beyond what he can bear. He gets rewarded for that (good) which he has earned, and he is punished for that (evil) which he has earned. Our Lord! Punish us not if we forget or fall into error. Our Lord! Lay not on us a burden like that which You did lay on those before us. Our Lord! Put not on us a burden greater than we have the strength to bear. Pardon us and grant us forgiveness. Have mercy on us. You are our Protector, and help us against the disbelieving people.The Messenger believes in what has been sent down to him from his Lord, and so do the believers. Each one believes in Allah, His Angels, His Books, and His Messengers. They say: 'We make no distinction between any of His Messengers,' and they say: 'We hear, and we obey. (We seek) Your Forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the return.' Allah burdens not a person beyond what he can bear. He gets rewarded for that (good) which he has earned, and he is punished for that (evil) which he has earned. Our Lord! Punish us not if we forget or fall into error. Our Lord! Lay not on us a burden like that which You did lay on those before us. Our Lord! Put not on us a burden greater than we have the strength to bear. Pardon us and grant us forgiveness. Have mercy on us. You are our Protector, and help us against the disbelieving people.
items.reference: Al-Baqarah 2:285-6. These two Verses will be sufficient for anyone who recites them at night before sleeping. Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari.9/94, Muslim 1/554. Hisn al-Muslim 101

items.arabic: بِاسْمِكَ رَبِّي وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِي، وَبِكَ أَرْفَعُهُ، فَإِن أَمْسَكْتَ نَفْسِي فارْحَمْهَا، وَإِنْ أَرْسَلْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا بِمَا تَحْفَظُ بِهِ عِبَادَكَ الصَّالِحِينَ
items.transliteration: Bismika Rabbī waḍa`tu janbī, wa bika arfa`uh, fa in amsakta nafsī farḥamhā, wa in arsaltahā faḥfaẓhā bimā taḥfaẓu bihi `ibādakaṣ-ṣāliḥīn.
items.english: With Your Name1 my Lord, I lay myself down; and with Your Name I rise. And if You take my soul, have mercy on it, and if You send it back then protect it as You protect Your righteous slaves.2
items.reference: 1 'If any of you rises from his bed and later returns to it, let him dust off his bed with his waist garment three times and mention the Name of Allah, for he does not know what may have entered the bed after him, and when he lies down he should say. . . '. 2 Al-Bukhari 1 1/ 126 and Muslim 4/2084. Hisn al-Muslim 102

items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ خَلَقْتَ نَفْسِي وَأَنْتَ تَوَفَّاهَا، لَكَ مَمَاتُهَا وَمَحْياهَا، إِنْ أَحْيَيْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، وَإِنْ أَمَتَّهَا فَاغْفِرْ لَهَا. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ العَافِيَةَ
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innaka khalaqta nafsī wa anta tawaffāhā, laka mamātuhā wa maḥyāhā, in aḥyaytahā faḥfaẓhā, wa in amattahā faghfir lahā. Allāhumma innī as'aluka ‘l-`āfiyah.
items.english: O Allah, You have created my soul and You take it back. Unto You is its death and its life. If You give it life then protect it, and if You cause it to die then forgive it. O Allah, I ask You for strength.
items.reference: Muslim 4/2083 and Ahmad 2/79. Hisn al-Muslim 103

items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ قِنـي عَذابَـكَ يَـوْمَ تَبْـعَثُ عِبـادَك .
items.transliteration: Allāhumma qinī `adhābaka yawma tab`athu `ibādak.
items.english: O Allah,1 save me from Your punishment on the Day that You resurrect Your slaves.2
items.reference: 1 'When the Prophet (ﷺ) wanted to lie down to sleep, he used to place his right hand under his cheek and say...' 2 Abu Dawud 4/311. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/143. Hisn al-Muslim 104

items.arabic: بِاسْـمِكَ اللّهُـمَّ أَمـوتُ وَأَحْـيا
items.transliteration: Bismika Allāhumma amūtu wa aḥyā.
items.english: In Your Name, O Allah, I die and I live.
items.reference: Here, dying and living are metaphors for sleep and wakefulness. This explains why the normal order of these words has been reversed in this Hadith. In other contexts the living is mentioned before dying. See Qur'an Al-Baqarah 2:258, Aal-'Imrdn 3:156, Al-A'raf 7:158 among many other examples, (trans.). See also Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/113, Muslim 4/ 2083. Hisn al-Muslim 105

items.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ (ثلاثاً وثلاثين) وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (ثلاثاً وثلاثين) وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ (أربعاً وثلاثينَ)
items.transliteration: Subḥānallāh, Wa ‘l-ḥamdu lillāh, Wallāhu Akbar.
items.english: Glory is to Allah (thirty-three times in Arabic), Praise is to Allah (thirty-three times), Allah is the Most Great (thirty-four times)
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 7/71, Muslim 4/2091. Hisn al-Muslim 106

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ رَبَّ السّمـواتِ السَّبْـعِ وَرَبَّ العَـرْشِ العَظـيم
رَبَّنـا وَرَبَّ كُـلِّ شَـيء
فالِـقَ الحَـبِّ وَالنَّـوى
وَمُـنَزِّلَ التَّـوْراةِ وَالإنْجـيل والفُـرْقان
أَعـوذُ بِـكَ مِن شَـرِّ كُـلِّ شَـيءٍ أَنْـتَ آخِـذٌ بِنـاصِـيَتِه
اللّهُـمَّ أَنْـتَ الأوَّلُ فَلَـيسَ قَبْـلَكَ شَيء
وَأَنْـتَ الآخِـرُ فَلَـيسَ بَعْـدَكَ شَيء
وَأَنْـتَ الظّـاهِـرُ فَلَـيْسَ فَـوْقَـكَ شَيء
وَأَنْـتَ الْبـاطِـنُ فَلَـيْسَ دونَـكَ شَيء
اقـْضِ عَنّـا الـدَّيْـنَ
وَأَغْـنِنـا مِنَ الفَـقْر
items.transliteration: Allāhumma Rabbas-samāwātis-sab`i wa Rabba ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-`Aẓīm, Rabbanā wa Rabba kulli shay', fāliqa ‘l-ḥabbi wan-nawā, wa munzilat-Tawrāti wal-'Injīli, wal-Furqān, a`ūdhu bika min sharri kulli shay'in anta ākhidhun bināṣiyatih. Allāhumma anta ‘l-awwalu falaysa qablaka shay', wa antal-ākhiru falaysa ba`daka shay', wa antaẓ-ẓāhiru falaysa fawqaka shay', wa antal-bāṭinu falaysa dūnaka shay', iqḍi `annad-dayn, wa aghninā mina ‘l-faqr.
items.english:
O Allah! Lord of the seven heavens and Lord of the Magnificent Throne.
Our Lord and the Lord of everything. Splitter of the grain and the date-stone,
Revealer of the Torah and the Injeel1 and the Furqan (the Qur'an),
I seek refuge in You from the evil of everything that You shall seize by the forelock.2
O Allah You are the First and nothing has come before you,
and You are the Last, and nothing may come after You.
You are the Most High, nothing is above You
and You are the Most Near, and nothing is nearer than You.
Remove our debts from us
and enrich us against poverty. 3
items.reference: 1 The Scripture that was revealed to 'Isa (Jesus). 2 See Qur'an Al-'Alaq 96:15, where seizure by the forelock preceeds being cast into Hell. (Translator) 3 Muslim 4/2084. Hisn al-Muslim 107

items.arabic:
الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا،
وَكَفَانَا، وَآوَانَا،
فَكَمْ مِمَّنْ لاَ كَافِيَ لَهُ وَلاَ مُؤْوِيَ
items.transliteration: Alḥamdu lillāhil-lathī 'aṭ`amanā wa saqānā, wa kafānā, wa 'āwānā, fakam mimman lā kāfiya lahu wa lā mu'wī.
items.english:
Praise is to Allah Who has provided us with food and with drink, sufficed us and gave us an abode,
for how many are there with no provision and no home.
items.reference: Muslim 4/2085. Hisn al-Muslim 108

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ عالِـمَ الغَـيْبِ وَالشّـهادَةِ
فاطِـرَ السّماواتِ وَالأرْضِ
رَبَّ كـلِّ شَـيءٍ وَمَليـكَه
أَشْهَـدُ أَنْ لا إِلـهَ إِلاّ أَنْت
أَعـوذُ بِكَ مِن شَـرِّ نَفْسـي
وَمِن شَـرِّ الشَّيْـطانِ وَشِـرْكِه
وَأَنْ أَقْتَـرِفَ عَلـى نَفْسـي سوءاً
أَوْ أَجُـرَّهُ إِلـى مُسْـلِم
items.transliteration: Allāhumma `ālima ‘l-ghaybi wash-shahādah, fātiras-samāwāti wa ‘l-ardh, Rabba kulli shay'in wa malīkah, ash-hadu an lā ilāha illā ant, a`ūdhu bika min sharri nafsī, wa min sharrish-shayṭāni wa shirkih, wa an aqtarifa `alā nafsī sū'an, aw ajurrahu ilā Muslim.
items.english:
O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the evident,
Maker of the heavens and the earth,
Lord of everything and its Master,
I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship but You.
I seek refuge in You from the evil of my
soul,
and from the evil of Satan and his helpers.
(I seek refuge in You) from bringing evil upon my soul
and from harming any Muslim.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 4/317. See also Al-Albani, SahihAt-Tirmidhi 3/142. Hisn al-Muslim 109

items.arabic:
يقرأ ((ألم * تنزيل)) السجدة،
و((تبارك الذي بيده الملك))
items.english: Recite Surah 32 (As-Sajdah) and Surah 67 (Al-Mulk) in Arabic.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi, An-Nasa'i. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami ' As-Saghir 4/255 Hisn al-Muslim 110

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ أَسْـلَمْتُ نَفْـسي إِلَـيْكَ،
وَفَوَّضْـتُ أَمْـري إِلَـيْكَ،
وَوَجَّـهْتُ وَجْـهي إِلَـيْكَ،
وَأَلْـجَـاْتُ ظَهـري إِلَـيْكَ،
رَغْبَـةً وَرَهْـبَةً إِلَـيْكَ،
لا مَلْجَـأَ وَلا مَنْـجـا مِنْـكَ إِلاّ إِلَـيْكَ،
آمَنْـتُ بِكِتـابِكَ الّـذي أَنْزَلْـتَ
وَبِنَبِـيِّـكَ الّـذي أَرْسَلْـت
items.transliteration: Allāhumma aslamtu nafsī ilayk, wa fawwaḍtu amrī ilayk, wa wajjahtu wajhī ilayk, wa alja'tu ẓahrī ilayk, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilayk, lā malja'a wa la manjā minka illā ilayk, āmantu bikitābika ‘l-ladhī anzalt, wa bi-nabiyyika ‘l-ladhī arsalt.
items.english:
O Allah,1 I submit myself to You,
entrust my affairs to You,
turn my face to You,
and lay myself down depending upon You,
hoping in You and fearing You.
There is no refuge, and no escape, except to You.
I believe in Your Book (the Qur'an) that You revealed,
and the Prophet whom You sent.
items.reference: 1 "Before you go to bed perform ablutions as you would for prayer, then lie down on your right side and say. . . " The Prophet (ﷺ) said: "Whoever says this and dies in his sleep, has died in a state of the natural monotheism (Fitrah)." Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/113, Muslim 4/2081. Hisn al-Muslim 111

cat29.title: When tossing and turning during the night / الدعاء إذا تقلب ليلاً

items.arabic:
لا إِلـهَ إِلاّ اللهُ
الـواحِدُ القَهّـار
رَبُّ السَّـمواتِ وَالأرْضِ وَما بَيْـنَهـما
العَزيـزُ الغَـفّار
items.transliteration: Lā ilāha illallāh al-Wāḥidul-Qahhār, Rabbus-samāwāti wa ‘l-arḍi wa mā baynahuma al-`Azīzul-Ghaffār.
items.english:
There is none worthy of worship but Allah,
the One, the Victorious,
Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them,
the All-Mighty, the All-Forgiving.
items.reference: This is to be said if you turn over in bed during the night. Al-Hakim graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed 1/540. Also see An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah, and Ibn As-Sunni. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 4/ 213. Hisn al-Muslim 112

cat30.title: Upon experiencing unrest, fear, apprehensiveness during sleep / دعاء القلق والفزع في النوم ومن بلي بالوحشة

items.arabic:
أَعـوذُ بِكَلِمـاتِ اللّهِ التّـامّـاتِ
مِن غَضَـبِهِ وَعِـقابِهِ وَشَـرِّ عِبـادِهِ
وَمِنْ هَمَـزاتِ الشَّـياطينِ وَأَنْ يَحْضـرون
items.transliteration: A`ūdhu bikalimāti ‘llāhit-tāmmāti min ghaḍabihi wa `iqābihi wa sharri `ibādih, wa min hamazātish-shayāṭīni wa an yaḥḍurūn.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 4/12. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At- Tirmidhi 3/171. Hisn al-Muslim 113

cat31.title: Upon seeing a good dream or a bad dream / ما يفعل من رأى الرؤيا أو الحلم

items.arabic:
يَنْفُثُ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ (ثلاثاً).
يَسْتَعِيذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا رَأَى (ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ).
لاَ يُحَدِّثْ بِهَا أَحَداً.
يَــتَحَوَّلُ عَنْ جَنْبِهِ الَّذِي كَانَ عَلَيْهِ.
items.english:
Spit to your left (three times).1

Seek refuge in Allah from the Devil and from the evil of what you have seen
(three times).2

Do not speak about it to anyone.3

Turn over on your other side.4
items.reference: 1. Muslim 4/1 772. 2. Muslim 4/1 772, 3. 3. Muslim 4/1772. 4. Muslim 4/1773. Hisn al-Muslim 114

items.arabic: يقوم يصلي إن أراد ذلك.ويَقُومُ يُصَلِّي إِنْ أَرَادَ ذَلِكَ.
items.english: Get up and pray if you desire to do so.
items.reference: Muslim 4/1773. Hisn al-Muslim 115

items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ اهْـدِنـي فـيمَنْ هَـدَيْـت، وَعـافِنـي فـيمَنْ عافَـيْت، وَتَوَلَّـني فـيمَنْ تَوَلَّـيْت، وَبارِكْ لـي فـيما أَعْطَـيْت، وَقِـني شَرَّ ما قَضَـيْت، فَإِنَّـكَ تَقْـضي وَلا يُقْـضى عَلَـيْك ، إِنَّـهُ لا يَـذِلُّ مَنْ والَـيْت، [ وَلا يَعِـزُّ مَن عـادَيْت ]، تَبـارَكْـتَ رَبَّـنا وَتَعـالَـيْت
items.transliteration: Allāhumma’hdinī fī man hadayt, wa `āfinī fī man `āfayt, wa tawallanī fī man tawallayt, wa bārik lī fī mā a`atayt, wa qinī sharra mā qaḍayt, fa innaka taqḍī wa lā yuqḍā `alayk, innahu lā yadhillu man wālayt, [wa lā ya`izzu man `ādayt] , tabārakta Rabbanā wa ta`ālayt.
items.english:
O Allah, guide me with those whom You have guided,
and strengthen me with those whom You have given strength.
Take me to Your care with those whom You have taken to Your care.
Bless me in what You have given me.
Protect me from the evil You have ordained.
Surely, You command and are not commanded,
and none whom You have committed to Your care shall be humiliated
[and none whom You have taken as an enemy shall taste glory].
You are Blessed, Our Lord, and Exalted.
items.reference: Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, An-Nasa'i, At-Tirmidhi, Ahmad, Ad-Darimi, Al-Hakim, and Al-Bayhaqi. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 1/144, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/194, and 'Irwa'ul-GhaW. 2/ 172. Hisn al-Muslim 116

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أَعـوذُ بِرِضـاكَ مِنْ سَخَطِـك،
وَبِمُعـافاتِـكَ مِنْ عُقوبَـتِك،
وَأَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْـك،
لا أُحْصـي ثَنـاءً عَلَـيْك،
أَنْـتَ كَمـا أَثْنَـيْتَ عَلـى نَفْسـِك
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innī a`ūdhu biriḍāka min sakhaṭik, wa bimu`āfātika min `uqūbatik, wa a`ūdhu bika mink, lā uḥṣī thanā'an `alayk, Anta kamā athnayta `alā nafsik.
items.english:
O Allah, I seek refuge with Your Pleasure from Your anger.
I seek refuge in Your forgiveness from Your punishment.
I seek refuge in You from You.
I cannot count Your praises,
You are as You have praised Yourself.
items.reference: Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, An-Nasa'i, At-Tirmidhi, Ahmad. See Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/180, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/194, and 'Irwa'ul-Ghalil. 2/ 175. Hisn al-Muslim 117

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِيّـاكَ نعْـبُدْ،
وَلَـكَ نُصَلّـي وَنَسْـجُد،
وَإِلَـيْكَ نَسْـعى وَنَحْـفِد،
نَـرْجو رَحْمَـتَك،
وَنَخْشـى عَـذابَك،
إِنَّ عَـذابَكَ بالكـافرين ملْحَـق.
اللّهُـمَّ إِنّا نَسْتَعـينُكَ
وَنَسْتَـغْفِرُك،
وَنُثْـنـي عَلَـيْك الخَـيْرَ،
وَلا نَكْـفُرُك،
وَنُـؤْمِنُ بِك،
وَنَخْـضَعُ لَكَ
وَنَخْـلَعُ مَنْ يَكْـفرُك
items.transliteration: Allāhumma iyyāka na`bud, wa laka nuṣallī wa nasjud, wa ilayka nas`ā wa naḥfid, narjū raḥmatak, wa nakhshā `adhābak, inna `adhābaka bilkāfirīna mulḥaq. Allāhumma innā nasta`īnuk, wa nastaghfiruk, wa nuthnī `alaykal-khayr, wa lā nakfuruk, wa nu'minu bik, wa nakhḍa`u lak, wa nakhla`u man yakfuruk.
items.english:
O Allah, You alone do we worship,
and to You we pray and bow down prostrate.
To You we hasten to worship and to serve.
Our hope is for Your mercy,
and we fear Your punishment.
Surely, Your punishment of the disbelievers is at hand.
O Allah, we seek Your help and Your forgiveness,
and we praise You beneficently.
We do not deny You and we believe in You.
We surrender to You
and renounce whoever disbelieves in You.
items.reference: Al-Bayhaqi graded its chain authentic in As-Sunan Al-Kubra. Al-Albani said in 'Irwa'ul-GhaliL 2/170 that its chain is authentic as a statement of 'Umar. Hisn al-Muslim 118

cat33.title: Immediately after salam of the witr prayer / الذكر عقب السلام من الوتر

items.arabic:
"سبحان الملك القدوس"
ثلاث مرات والثالثة يجهر بها ويمد بها صوته
"ربِّ الملائكةِ والرّوح"
items.transliteration: Subḥāna ‘l-Maliki ‘l-Quddūs. [Recite three times, and raise and extend the voice on the third time and say...] Rabbi ‘l-Malā'ikati war-rūh.
items.english:
Glory is to the King, the Holy.
[Recite three times in Arabic, and raise and extend the voice on the third time and say...]
Lord of the angels and the Spirit.
items.reference: An-Nasa'i 3/244, Ad-Daraqutni and others. The final addition is from Ad-Daraqutni's version 2/ 31 and its chain of narration is authentic. See the checking of Zadul-Ma'ad by Shu'aib Al-Arna'ut and 'Abdul-Qadir Al-Arna'ut 1/337. Hisn al-Muslim 119

cat34.title: For anxiety and sorrow / دعاء الهم والحزن

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّي عَبْـدُكَ
ابْنُ عَبْـدِكَ
ابْنُ أَمَتِـكَ
نَاصِيَتِي بِيَـدِكَ
مَاضٍ فِيَّ حُكْمُكَ
عَدْلٌ فِيَّ قَضَاؤكَ
أَسْأَلُـكَ بِكُلِّ اسْمٍ هُوَ لَكَ
سَمَّـيْتَ بِهِ نَفْسَكَ
أِوْ أَنْزَلْتَـهُ فِي كِتَابِكَ
أَوْ عَلَّمْـتَهُ أَحَداً مِنْ خَلْقِـكَ
أَوِ اسْتَـأْثَرْتَ بِهِ فِي عِلْمِ الغَيْـبِ عِنْـدَكَ
أَنْ تَجْـعَلَ القُرْآنَ رَبِيـعَ قَلْبِـي
وَنورَ صَـدْرِي
وجَلَاءَ حُـزْنِي
وذَهَابَ هَمِّـي
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innī `abduk, ibnu `abdik, ibnu amatik, nāsiyatī biyadik, māḍin fiyya ḥukmuk, `adlun fiyya qaḍā'uk, as'aluka bikullis’min huwa lak, sammayta bihi nafsak, aw anzaltahu fī kitābik, aw `allamtahu aḥadan min khalqik, aw‘ista'tharta bihi fī `ilmil-ghaybi `indak, an taj`ala ‘l-Qur'āna rabī`a qalbī, wa nūra ṣadrī, wa jalā'a ḥuznī, wa dhahāba hammī.
items.english:
O Allah, I am Your slave,
and the son of Your male slave,
and the son of your female slave.
My forehead is in Your Hand (i.e. you have control over me).
Your Judgment upon me is assured,
and Your Decree concerning me is just.
I ask You by every Name that You have named Yourself with,
revealed in Your Book,
taught any one of Your creation,
or kept unto Yourself in the knowledge of the unseen that is with You,
to make the Qur'an the spring of my heart,
and the light of my chest,
the banisher of my sadness,
and the reliever of my distress.
items.reference: Ahmad 1/391, and Al-Albani graded it authentic. Hisn al-Muslim 120

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّي أَعْوذُ بِكَ مِنَ
الهَـمِّ وَ الْحُـزْنِ،
والعًجْـزِ والكَسَلِ
والبُخْـلِ والجُـبْنِ،
وضَلْـعِ الـدَّيْنِ
وغَلَبَـةِ الرِّجال
items.transliteration: Allāhumma 'innī 'a`ūdhu bika mina ‘l-ḥammi wa ‘l-ḥuzn, wa ‘l-`ajzi wa ‘l-kasal, wa ‘l-bukhli wa ‘l-jubn, wa ḍala`id-dayn, wa ghalabatir-rijāl.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 7/158. See also Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/173. Hisn al-Muslim 121

cat35.title: For one in distress / دعاء الكرب

items.arabic:
لَا إلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ
الْعَظـيمُ الْحَلِـيمْ،
لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ
رَبُّ العَـرْشِ العَظِيـمِ،
لَا إِلَـهَ إِلَّا اللَّهْ
رَبُّ السَّمَـوّاتِ ورّبُّ الأَرْضِ
ورَبُّ العَرْشِ الكَـريم
items.transliteration: Lā ilāha illallāh al-`Aẓīmul-Ḥalīm, lā ilāha illallāh Rabbu ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-'Aẓīm, lā ilāha illallāh Rabbus-samāwāti wa Rabbu ‘l-arḍ wa Rabbu ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-Karīm.
items.english:
There is none worthy of worship but Allah,
the Mighty, the Forbearing.
There is none worthy of worship but Allah,
Lord of the Magnificent Throne.
There is none worthy of worship but Allah,
Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth,
and Lord of the Noble Throne.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 8/154, Muslim 4/2092. Hisn al-Muslim 122

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ رَحْمَتَـكَ أَرْجـو
فَلا تَكِلـني إِلى نَفْـسي طَـرْفَةَ عَـيْن،
وَأَصْلِـحْ لي شَأْنـي كُلَّـه
لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أنْـت
items.transliteration: Allāhumma raḥmataka arjū falā takilnī ilā nafsī ṭarfata `ayn, wa aṣliḥ lī sha'nī kullah, lā ilāha illā ant.
items.english:
O Allah, I hope for Your mercy.
Do not leave me to myself even for the blinking of an eye (i.e. a moment).
Correct all of my affairs for me.
There is none worthy of worship but You.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 4/324, Ahmad 5/42. Al-Albani graded it as good in Sahih Abu Dawud 3/959. Hisn al-Muslim 123

items.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أنْـت سُـبْحانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْـتُ مِنَ الظّـالِميـن
items.transliteration: Lā ilāha illā anta subḥānaka innī kuntu minaẓ-ẓālimīn.
items.english:
There is none worthy of worship but You, glory is to You.
Surely, I was among the wrongdoers.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/529. Al-Hakim declared it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him 1/ 505. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/168. Hisn al-Muslim 124

items.arabic: اللهُ اللهُ رَبِّي لا أُشْـرِكُ بِهِ شَيْـئاً
items.transliteration: Allāh, Allāhu Rabbī lā ushriku bihi shay'a.
items.english: Allah, Allah is my Lord. I do not associate anything with Him.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 2/87. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Mdjah 2/335. Hisn al-Muslim 125

cat36.title: Upon encountering an enemy or those of authority / دعاء لقاء العدو وذي السلطان

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِنا نَجْـعَلُكَ في نُحـورِهِـم،
وَنَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شُرورِهـمْ
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innā naj`aluka fī nuḥūrihim wa na`ūdhu bika min shurūrihim.
items.english:
O Allah, we ask You to restrain them by their necks,
and we seek refuge in You from their evil.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 2/89, and Al-Hakim graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed 2/142. Hisn al-Muslim 126

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ أَنْتَ عَضُـدي، وَأَنْتَ نَصـيري،
بِكَ أَجـولُ وَبِكَ أَصـولُ وَبِكَ أُقـاتِل
items.transliteration: Allāhumma anta `aḍudī, wa anta naṣīrī, bika ajūlu, wa bika aṣūlu, wa bika uqātil.
items.english:
O Allah, You are my strength and You are my support.
For Your sake, I go forth and for Your sake, I advance and for Your sake, I fight.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 3/42, At-Tirmidhi 5/572. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/183. Hisn al-Muslim 127

items.arabic: حَسْبُـنا اللهُ وَنِعْـمَ الوَكـيل
items.transliteration: Ḥasbunallāhu wa ni`mal-wakīl.
items.english: Allah is sufficient for us and the best of those on whom to depend.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, 5/172. Hisn al-Muslim 128

cat37.title: For one afraid of the ruler's injustice / دعاء من خاف ظلم السلطان

items.arabic:
اللَّهُمَّ ربَّ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ،
وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ،
كُنْ لِي جَاراً مِنْ فُلاَنِ بْنِ فُلاَنٍ،
وَأَحْزَابِهِ مِنْ خَلاَئِقِكَ،
أَنْ يَفْرُطَ عَلَيَّ أَحَدٌ مِنْهُمْ أَوْ يَطْغَى،
عَزَّ جَارُكَ،
وَجَلَّ ثَنَاؤُكَ،
وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
items.transliteration: Allāhumma Rabbas-samāwātis-sab`, wa Rabba ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-`Aẓīm, kun lī jāran min [here you mention the person's name], wa aḥzābihi min khalā'iqik, an yafruṭa `alayya aḥadun minhum aw yaṭghā, `azza jāruk, wa jalla thanā'uk, wa lā ilāha illā ant.
items.english:
O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens,
Lord of the Magnificent Throne,
be for me a support against [such and such a person] and his helpers from among your creatures,
lest any of them abuse me or do me wrong.
Mighty is Your patronage,
and glorious are Your praises.
There is none worthy of worship but You.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, Al-'Adab Al-Mufrad (no. 707). Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Al-'Adab Al-Mufrad (no. 545). Hisn al-Muslim 129

items.arabic:
الله أكبر،
الله أعز من خلقه جميعاً ،
الله أعز مما أخاف وأحذر ،
أعوذ بالله الذي لا إله إلا هو ،
الممسك السموات السبع
أن يقعن على الأرض إلا بإذنه ،
من شر عبدك فلان ،
وجنوده وأتباعه وأشياعه ،
من الجن والأنس ،
اللهم كن لي جاراً من شرهم ،
جل ثناؤك وعز جارك ،
وتبارك اسمك ،
ولا إله غيرك
(ثلاث مرات)
items.transliteration: Allāhu Akbar, Allāhu a`azzu min khalqihi jamī`a, Allāhu a`azzu mimmā akhāfu wa aḥdhar, a`ūdhu billāhi ‘l-ladhī lā ilāha illā hū, almumsikis-samāwātis-sab`i an yaqa`na `ala ‘l-arḍi illā bi idhnih, min sharri `abdika [name of the person], wa junūdihi wa atbā`ihi wa ashyā`ih, mina ‘l-jinni wa ‘l-ins, Allāhumma kun lī jāran min sharrihim, jalla thanā'uk, wa `azza jāruk, wa tabāraka-smuk, wa lā ilāha ghayruk.
items.english:
Allah is the Most Great,
Mightier than all His creation.
He is Mightier than what I fear and dread.
I seek refuge in Allah, Who there is none worthy of worship but Him.
He is the One Who holds the seven heavens from falling upon the earth except by His command.
[I seek refuge in You Allah] from the evil of Your slave [name of the person],
and his helpers, his followers, and his supporters from among the jinn and mankind.
O Allah, be my support against their evil.
Glorious are Your praises
and mighty is Your patronage.
Blessed is Your Name,
there is no true God but You.
(Recite three times in Arabic.)
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, 5/172. Hisn al-Muslim 130

items.arabic:
اللَّهُمَّ مُنْزِلَ الْكِتَاب
سَرِيعَ الْحِسَاب
اهْزِم الأَحْزَاب
اللَّهُمَّ اهْزِمْهُمْ وَ زَلْزِلْهُم
items.transliteration: Allāhumma munzila ‘l-kitāb, sarī`a ‘l-ḥisāb, ihzimi ‘l-aḥzāb, Allāhumma-hzimhum wa zalzilhum.
items.english:
O Allah, Revealer of the Book,
Swift to account,
defeat the groups (of disbelievers).
O Allah, defeat them and shake them.
items.reference: Muslim 3/1362. Hisn al-Muslim 131

cat39.title: When being afraid of a group of people / ما يقول من خاف قوماً

items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِيهِم بمَا شِئْت
items.transliteration: Allāhummak-finīhim bimā shi't.
items.english: O Allah, suffice (i.e. protect) me against them however You wish.
items.reference: Muslim 4/2300. Hisn al-Muslim 132

cat40.title: For one afflicted with doubt in his faith / دعاء من أصابه شك في الإيمان

items.arabic:
يَسْتَعِيذُ بِاللَّهِ.

يَنْتَهِي عَمَّا شك فِيهِ.
items.transliteration: A`ūdhu billāh
items.english:
(Say:
) I seek refuge in Allah. (Then you should desist from doing what you are in doubt about.)
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 6/336, Muslim 1/120. Hisn al-Muslim 133

items.arabic: يقول" آمنت بالله ورسله"
items.transliteration: Āmantu billāhi wa Rusulih.
items.english: Say: I believe in Allah and His Messengers.
items.reference: Muslim, 1/119-20. Hisn al-Muslim 134

items.arabic:
يقرأ قوله تعالى ((هُوَ الأوَّلُ، وَالآخِـرُ،
وَالظّـاهِـرُ، وَالْبـاطِـنُ،
وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيءٍ عَلـيم))
items.transliteration: Huwa ‘l-Awwalu wa ‘l-Ākhir, waẓ-Ẓāhiru wal-Bāṭin, wa huwa bikulli shay'in `Alīm.
items.english:
(Recite the Ayat) He is the First and the Last,
the Most High and the Most Near.
And He is the Knower of all things (in Arabic).
items.reference: Al-Hadid 57:3, Abu Dawud 4/329. Al-Albani graded it good in SahihAbu Dawud, 3/962. Hisn al-Muslim 135

cat41.title: Settling a debt / الدعاء قضاء الدين

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ اكْفِـني بِحَلالِـكَ عَنْ حَـرامِـك،
وَأَغْنِـني بِفَضْـلِكَ عَمَّـنْ سِـواك
items.transliteration: Allāhummak-finī biḥalālika `an ḥarāmik, wa 'aghninī bi faḍlika `amman siwāk.
items.english:
O Allah, suffice me with what You have allowed instead of what You have forbidden,
and make me independent of all others besides You.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/560. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/180. Hisn al-Muslim 136

items.arabic:
اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ،
وَالْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ،
وَالْبُخْلِ وَالْجُبْنِ،
وَضَلَعِ الدَّيْنِ وَغَلَبَةِ الرِّجَالِ
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innī a`ūdhu bika mina ‘l-ḥammi wa ‘l-ḥazan, wa ‘l-`ajzi wa ‘l-kasal, wa ‘l-bukhli wa ‘l-jubn, wa ḍala`id-dayni wa ghalabatir-rijāl.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 7/158. Hisn al-Muslim 137

cat42.title: For one afflicted by whisperings in prayer or recitation / دعاء الوسوسة في الصلاة والقراءة

items.arabic:
أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ،
وَاتْفُلْ عَلَى يَسَارِكَ (ثلاثاً)
items.transliteration: A`ūdhu billāhi minash-shayṭānir-rajīm.
items.english: Say: I seek refuge in Allah from Satan the outcast (then blow with a little spittle to your left). (Do this three times reciting in Arabic.)
items.reference: Muslim 4/1729. Hisn al-Muslim 138

cat43.title: For one whose affairs have become difficult / دعاء من استصعب عليه أمر

items.arabic:
اللَّهُمَّ لاَ سَهْلَ إِلاَّ مَا جَعَلْتَهُ سَهْلاً،
وَأَنْتَ تَجْعَلُ الْحَزْنَ إِذَا شِئْتَ سَهْلاً
items.transliteration: Allāhumma lā saḥla illā ma ja`altahu saḥla wa anta taj`alu ‘l-ḥazna idhā shi'ta saḥla.
items.english:
O Allah, there is no ease other than what You make easy.
If You please You ease sorrow.
items.reference: Ibn Hibban in his Sahih (no. 2427), and Ibn As- Sunni (no. 351). Al-Hafidh (Ibn Hajar) said that this Hadith is authentic. It was also declared authentic by 'Abdul-Qadir Al-Arna'ut in his checking of An-Nawawi's Kitabul-Athkarp. 106. Hisn al-Muslim 139

cat44.title: Upon committing a sin / ما يقول ويفعل من أذنب ذنباً

items.arabic: مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يُذنِبُ ذَنْباً فَيُحْسِنُ الطُّهُورَ، ثُمَّ يَقُومُ فَيُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ إِلاَّ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ
items.english: There is not any slave of Allah who commits a sin, then he perfects his purification and stands to pray two Rak'ahs of prayer, then seeks Allah's forgiveness, except that Allah will forgive him.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 2/86, At-Tirmidhi 2/257. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 1/283. Hisn al-Muslim 140

cat45.title: For expelling the devil and his whisperings / دعاء طرد الشيطان ووساوسه

items.arabic: الاستعاذة بالله منه
items.transliteration: A`ūdhu billāhi minash-shayṭānir-rajīm.
items.english: Seeking refuge with Allah against him (i.e. by saying I seek refuge in Allah from Satan the outcast).
items.reference: Abu Dawud 1/206, At-Tirmidhi. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 1/77, and Surat Al-Mu'minun, 23:98-9. Hisn al-Muslim 141

items.arabic: الْأَذَانُ
items.english: The call to prayer - 'Athan.
items.reference: Muslim 1/291, Al-Bukhari 1/151. Hisn al-Muslim 142

items.arabic: الْأَذْكَارُ وَقِرَاءَةُ الْقُرْآنِ
items.english: Saying words of Allah's remembrance (Thikr) and recitation of the Qur'an.
items.reference: "Do not turn your homes into graveyards, surely the Devil flees from the house in which Surat Al-Baqarah is read," Muslim 1/539. The Devil is also driven out by the invocations for morning and evening, those that are said before sleeping and upon waking up, those for entering and leaving the house, including those for entering and leaving the mosque, and by many other authentic invocations taught to us by the Prophet (ﷺ) such as the reading of 'Ayatul-Kursi, (Al-Baqarah 2:255), and the last two 'Ayat of Surat Al-Baqarah before going to sleep. Whoever says: "There is none worthy of worship but Allah alone, Who has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise, and he is Able to do all things," one hundred times, it will be a protection for him from the Devil throughout the day.' Hisn al-Muslim 143

cat46.title: When stricken with a mishap or overtaken by an affair / الدعاء حينما يقع مالا يرضاه أو غلب على أمره

items.arabic: قَدَّرَ اللهُ وَما شـاءَ فَعَـل
items.transliteration: Qaddarallāhu wa mā shā'a fa`al.
items.english: It is the Decree of Allah and He does whatever He wills.
items.reference: "The strong believer is better and more dear to Allah than the weak believer, and in each of them there is good. Be vigilant for what is to your benefit and seek the help of Allah and do not falter. But when you are striken by some setback, do not say: 'If only I had done such and such,' rather say: 'It is the Decree of Allah and He does whatever He wills.' For verily the saying 'if (i.e. if only I had) begins the work of the Devil." Muslim 4/2052. Hisn al-Muslim 144

cat47.title: Congratulation on the occasion of a birth and its reply / تهنئة المولود له وجوابه

items.arabic:
(بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ فِي الْمَوْهُوبِ لَكَ،
وَشَكَرْتَ الْوَاهِبَ،
وَبَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ،
وَرُزِقْتَ بِرَّهُ).


وَيَرُدُّ عَلَيْهِ الْمُهَــــــنَّأُ فَيَقُولُ:
(بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ وَبَارَكَ عَلَيْكَ،
وَجَزَاكَ اللَّهُ خَيْراً،
وَرَزَقَكَ اللَّهُ مِثْلَهُ،
وَأَجْزَلَ ثَوَابَكَ).
items.transliteration: Bārakallāhu laka fi ‘l-mawhūbi lak, wa shakarta ‘l-wāhib, wa balagha ashuddah, wa ruziqta birrah. The reply of the person being congratulated is to say: Bārakallahu laka wa bāraka `alayk, wa jazākallāhu khayra, wa razaqakallāhu mithlah, wa ajzala thawābak.
items.english:
May Allah bless you with His gift to you,
and may you (the new parent) give thanks,
may the child reach the maturity of years,
and may you be granted its righteousness.

The reply of the person being congratulated is to say:

May Allah bless you, and shower His blessings upon you,
and may Allah reward you well
and bestow upon you its like
and reward you abundantly
items.reference: An-Nawawi, Kitdbul-'Athkarp. 349, and Sahihul-'Athkar2/7l3 by Saleem Al-Hilali. Hisn al-Muslim 145

cat48.title: Placing childen under Allah’s protection / ما يعوذ به الأولاد

items.arabic:
كان رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يعوذ الحسن والحسين
" أعيذكما بكلمات الله التامة ،
من كل شيطان وهامة ،
ومن كل عينِ لامة "
items.transliteration: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to seek Allah's protection for Al-Hasan and Al-Husain by saying: U`īthukumā bikalimāti ‘llāhit-tāmmati min kulli shayṭānin wa hāmmah, wa min kulli `aynin lāmmah
items.reference: Al-Bukhari4/119. Hisn al-Muslim 146

cat49.title: When visiting the sick / الدعاء للمريض في عيادته

items.arabic: لا بأْسَ طَهـورٌ إِنْ شـاءَ الله
items.transliteration: Lā ba'sa ṭahūrun in shā Allāh.
items.english: Do not worry, it will be a purification (for you), Allah willing.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani Fathul-Bari 10/118. Hisn al-Muslim 147

items.arabic:
أَسْـأَلُ اللهَ العَـظيـم، رَبَّ العَـرْشِ العَـظيـم أَنْ يَشْفـيك .
(سبع مرات)
items.transliteration: As'alullāha ‘l-`Aẓīma Rabba ‘l-`Arshil-`Aẓīmi an yashfiyak.
items.english:
I ask Almighty Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne, to make you well.
(Recite seven times in Arabic .)
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/210 and Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 5/180.

cat50.title: Excellence of visiting the sick / فضل عيادة المريض

items.arabic: قَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: (إِذَا عَادَ الرَّجُلُ أَخَاهُ الْمُسْلِمَ مَشَى فِي خِرَافَةِ الْجَنَّةِ حَتَّى يَجْلِسَ، فَإِذَا جَلَسَ غَمَرَتْهُ الرَّحْمَةُ، فَإِنْ كَانَ غُدْوَةً صَلَّى عَلَيْهِ سَبْعُونَ أَلْفَ مَلَكٍ حَتَّى يُمْسِيَ، وَإِنْ كَانَ مَسَاءً صَلَّى عَلَيْهِ سَبْعُونَ أَلْفَ مَلَكٍ حَتَّى يُصْبِحَ)
items.english: When a man goes to visit his sick Muslim brother, he walks along a path of Paradise until he sits, and when he sits he is cloaked in mercy. If he comes in the morning, seventy thousand angels pray for him until evening, and if he comes in the evening, seventy thousand angels pray for him until morning.
items.reference: Hisn al-Muslim 149

cat51.title: When the sick have renounced all hope of life / دعاء المريض الذي يئس من حياته

items.arabic:
أللّهُـمَّ اغْفِـرْ لي وَارْحَمْـني
وَأَلْحِقْـني بِالرَّفـيقِ الأّعْلـى
items.transliteration: Allāhumma’ghfir lī warḥamnī wa alḥiqnī bir-rafīqi ‘l-'A`alā.
items.english:
O Allah, forgive me and have mercy upon me,
and join me with the highest companions (in Paradise).
items.reference: Al-Bukhari7/10, Muslim 4/1893. Hisn al-Muslim 150

items.arabic:
جعل النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم عند موته يدخل يديه في الماء فيمسح بهما وجههُ ويقول :
لا إله إلا الله إن للموت لسكرات
items.transliteration: As he was dying, the Prophet (ﷺ) dipped his hands in water and wiped his face saying: Lā ilāha illallāh inna li ‘lmawti la’sakarāt.
items.english:
There is none worthy of worship but Allah,
surely death has agonies.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 8/144. The Hadith also mention him using the Siivak (tooth stick). Hisn al-Muslim 151

items.arabic:
لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ
وَاللّهُ أَكْبَـر،
لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وحْـدَهُ،
لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ
وحْـدَهُ لا شَريكَ لهُ،
لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ
لهُ المُلكُ ولهُ الحَمْد،
لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ
وَلا حَـوْلَ وَلا قُـوَّةَ إِلاّ بِالله
items.transliteration: Lā ilāha illallāh wallāhu Akbar, lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdah, lā ilāha illallāh waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lā ilāha illallāh lahu ‘l-mulku wa lahu ‘l-ḥamd, lā ilāha illallāh wa lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāh.
items.english:
There is none worthy of worship but Allah,
Allah is the Most Great.
None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone.
None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone,
Who has no partner.
There is none worthy of worship but Allah,
His is the dominion and His is the praise.
There is none worthy of worship but Allah,
there is no power and no might but by Allah.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi and Ibn Majah. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/152 and Sahih Ibn Majah 2/317. Hisn al-Muslim 152

cat52.title: Instruction for the one nearing death / تلقين المحتضر لا إله إلا الله

items.arabic: من كان آخر كلامه "لا إله إلا الله" دخل الجنة
items.transliteration: Lā ilāha illallāh
items.english:
Whoever dies with the last words (whose meaning is):
"There is none worthy of worship but Allah" will enter Paradise.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 3/190. See also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 5/432. Hisn al-Muslim 153

cat53.title: For one afflicted by a calamity / دعاء من أصيب بمصيبة

items.arabic:
إِنّا للهِ وَإِنَا إِلَـيْهِ راجِعـون ،
اللهُـمِّ اْجُـرْني في مُصـيبَتي،
وَاخْلُـفْ لي خَيْـراً مِنْـها
items.transliteration: Innā lillāhi wa innā ilayhi rāji`ūn, Allāhumma-jurni fī muṣībatī wa 'khluf lī khayran minhā.
items.english:
We are from Allah and unto Him we return.
O Allah take me out of my plight
and bring to me after it something better.
items.reference: Muslim 2/632. Hisn al-Muslim 154

cat54.title: When closing the eyes of the deceased / الدعاء عند إغماض الميت

items.arabic:
اللهُـمِّ اغْفِـرْ لِـفلان (باسـمه)
وَارْفَعْ دَرَجَتَـهُ في المَهْـدِييـن ،
وَاخْـلُفْـهُ في عَقِـبِهِ في الغابِـرين،
وَاغْفِـرْ لَنـا وَلَـهُ يا رَبَّ العـالَمـين،
وَافْسَـحْ لَهُ في قَبْـرِهِ وَنَـوِّرْ لَهُ فيه
items.transliteration: Allāhumma’ghfir li (name of the person) warfa` darajatahu fi ‘l-mahdiyyīn, wakhlufhu fī `aqibihi fi ‘l-ghābirīn, wagh’fir-lanā wa lahu yā Rabba ‘l-`ālamīn, wafsaḥ lahu fī qabrihi wa nawwir lahu fīh.
items.english:
O Allah, forgive [name of the person]
and elevate his station among those who are guided.
Send him along the path of those who came before,
and forgive us and him, O Lord of the worlds.
Enlarge for him his grave, and shed light upon him in it.
items.reference: Muslim 2/634. Hisn al-Muslim 155

cat55.title: For the deceased at the funeral prayer / الدعاء للميت في الصلاة عليه

items.arabic: اللهُـمِّ اغْفِـرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْـه ، وَعافِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنْـه ، وَأَكْـرِمْ نُزُلَـه ، وَوَسِّـعْ مُدْخَـلَه ، وَاغْسِلْـهُ بِالْمـاءِ وَالثَّـلْجِ وَالْبَـرَدْ ، وَنَقِّـهِ مِنَ الْخطـايا كَما نَـقّيْتَ الـثَّوْبَ الأَبْيَـضَ مِنَ الدَّنَـسْ ، وَأَبْـدِلْهُ داراً خَـيْراً مِنْ دارِه ، وَأَهْلاً خَـيْراً مِنْ أَهْلِـه ، وَزَوْجَـاً خَـيْراً مِنْ زَوْجِه ، وَأَدْخِـلْهُ الْجَـنَّة ، وَأَعِـذْهُ مِنْ عَذابِ القَـبْر [وَعَذابِ النّـار]
items.transliteration: Allāhumma’ghfir lahu warḥamh, wa `āfihi, wa`fu `anh, wa akrim nuzulah, wa wassi` mudkhalah, wagh’silhu bi ‘lmā'i wath-thalji walbarad, wa naqqihi mina ‘l-khaṭāyā kamā naqqaytath-thawba ‘l-abyaḍa minad-danas, wa abdilhu dāran khayran min dārih, wa ahlan khayran min ahlih, wa zawjan khayran min zawjih, wa adkhilhu ‘l-jannah, wa a`idhhu min `adhābi ‘l-qabri [wa `adhābin-nār].
items.english:
O Allah, forgive him and have mercy on him,
and give him strength and pardon him.
Be generous to him,
and cause his entrance to be wide,
and wash him with water and snow and hail.
Cleanse him of his transgressions
as white cloth is cleansed of stains.
Give him an abode better than his home,
and a family better than his family,
and a wife better than his wife.
Take him into Paradise,
and protect him from the punishment of the grave [and from the punishment of Hell-fire].
items.reference: Muslim 2/663. Hisn al-Muslim 156

items.arabic:
اللهُـمِّ اغْفِـرْ لِحَيِّـنا وَمَيِّتِـنا،
وَشـاهِدِنا وَغائِبِـنا ،
وَصَغيـرِنا وَكَبيـرِنا ،
وَذَكَـرِنا وَأُنْثـانا .
اللهُـمِّ مَنْ أَحْيَيْـتَهُ مِنّا
فَأَحْيِـهِ عَلى الإِسْلام ،
وَمَنْ تَوَفَّـيْتَهُ مِنّا فَتَوَفَّـهُ عَلى الإِيـمان ،
اللهُـمِّ لا تَحْـرِمْنـا أَجْـرَه ،
وَلا تُضِـلَّنا بَعْـدَه
items.transliteration: Allāhumma’ghfir liḥayyinā, wa mayyitinā, wa shāhidinā, wa ghā'ibinā, wa ṣaghīrinā wa kabīrinā, wa dhakarinā wa unthānā. Allāhumma man aḥyaytahu minnā fa aḥyihi `ala ‘l-Islām, wa man tawaffaytahu minnā fatawaffahu `alal-īmān, Allāhumma lā taḥrimnā ajrah wa lā tuḍillanā ba`dah.
items.english:
O Allah forgive our living and our dead,
those who are with us and those who are absent,
our young and our old,
our menfolk and our womenfolk.
O Allah, whomever you give life from among us
give him life in Islam,
and whomever you take away from us
take him away in Faith.
O Allah, do not forbid us their reward
and do not send us astray after them.
items.reference: Ibn Majah 1/480, Ahmad 2/368. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/251. Hisn al-Muslim 157

items.arabic:
اللهُـمِّ إِنَّ فُلانَ بْنَ فُلانٍ في ذِمَّـتِك ،
وَحَبْـلِ جِـوارِك ،
فَقِـهِ مِنْ فِتْـنَةِ الْقَـبْرِ وَعَذابِ النّـار ،
وَأَنْتَ أَهْلُ الْوَفـاءِ وَالْـحَقِّ ،
فَاغْفِـرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْـهُ ،
إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الغَـفورُ الـرَّحيم
items.transliteration: Allāhumma inna [name the person] fī dhimmatik, wa ḥabli jiwārik, faqihi min fitnati ‘l-qabri wa `adhābin-nār, wa anta ahlu ‘l-wafā'i wa ‘l-ḥaqq. Faghfir lahu warḥamh innaka anta ‘l-Ghafūrur-Raḥīm.
items.english:
O Allah, surely [name the person] is under Your protection,
and in the rope of Your security,
so save him from the trial of the grave and from the punishment of the Fire.
You fulfill promises and grant rights,
so forgive him and have mercy on him.
Surely You are Most Forgiving, Most Merciful.
items.reference: Ibn Majah, Abu Dawud 3/211. See also Al Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/251. Hisn al-Muslim 158

items.arabic:
اللهُـمِّ عَبْـدُكَ وَابْنُ أَمَـتِك،
احْتـاجَ إِلى رَحْمَـتِك،
وَأَنْتَ غَنِـيٌّ عَنْ عَذابِـه،
إِنْ كانَ مُحْـسِناً فَزِدْ في حَسَـناتِه،
وَإِنْ كانَ مُسـيئاً فَتَـجاوَزْ عَنْـه
items.transliteration: Allāhumma `abduka wabnu 'amatik iḥtāja ilā raḥmatik, wa anta ghaniyyun `an `adhābih, in kāna muḥsinan fazid fī ḥasanātih, wa in kāna musī'an fatajāwaz `anh.
items.english:
O Allah, Your male slave and the child of Your female slave is in need of Your mercy,
and You are not in need of his torment.
If he was pious then increase his rewards
and if he was a transgressor then pardon him.
items.reference: Al-Hakim 1/359 who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him. See also Al-Albani, Ahkamul-Jana'iz, p. 125. Hisn al-Muslim 159

cat56.title: When the deceased is a child, during the funeral prayer / الدعاء للفرط في الصلاة عليه

items.arabic:
"اللهم أعذه من عذاب القبر "


وإن قال:
"اللهم اجعله فرطاً وذخراً لوالديه ،
وشفيعاً مجاباً .
اللهم ثقل به موازينها
وأعظم به أجورهما ،
وألحقهُ بصالح المؤمنين ،
واجعلهُ في كفالة إبراهيم ،
وقه برحمتك عذاب الجحيم ،
وأبدله داراً خيراً من داره ،
وأهلاً خيراً من أهله ،
اللهم اغفر لاسلافنا ، وأفراطنا ،
ومن سبقنا بالإيمان "
فحسن
items.transliteration: Allāhumma a`idh’hu min `adhābi ‘l-qabr [or say:] Allāhumma ‘j`alhu faraṭan wa dhukhran liwālidayh, wa shafī`an mujāban. Allāhumma thaqqil bihi mawāzīnahumā wa a`ẓim bihi ujūrahumā, wa alḥiqhu biṣāliḥi ‘l-mu'minīn, waj`alhu fī kafālati Ibrāhīm, wa qihi biraḥmatika `adhāba ‘l-jaḥīm, wa abdilhu dāran khayran min dārih, wa ahlan khayran min ahlih, Allāhumma’ghfir li aslāfinā wa afrāṭinā wa man sabaqanā bil īmān.
items.english:
O Allah, protect him from the torment of the grave.
[It is also good to say:]
O Allah, make him a precursor, a forerunner and a treasure for his parents and an answered intercessor.
O Allah, make him weigh heavily in their scales (of good) and magnify their reward.
Make him join the righteous of the believers.
Place him in the care of Ibrahim.
Save him by Your mercy from the torment of Hell.
Give him a home better than his home,
and a family better than his family.
O Allah, forgive those who have gone (i.e. passed away) before us, our children lost (by death), and those who have preceded us in Faith.
items.reference: Ibn Qudamah, Al-Mughni 3/416 and Ad-Duroosul-Muhimmah li-Aammatil-'Ummah, pg. 15, by Shaikh 'Abdul-'Aziz bin Baz. Hisn al-Muslim 160

items.arabic: اللهُـمِّ اجْعَلْـهُ لَنا فَرَطـاً، وَسَلَـفاً وَأَجْـراً
items.transliteration: Allāhumma’j`alhu lanā faraṭan, wa salafan, wa ajra.
items.english: O Allah, make him for us a precursor, a forerunner and a cause of reward.
items.reference: Al-Hasan (Al-Basri) used to recite Surat Al-Fatihah for a child's funeral and then say this. Al-Bukhari, Kitabul-Jana'iz, p. 65. Hisn al-Muslim 161

items.arabic:
"إن لله ما أخذ
وله ما أعطى .
وكل شيء عنده بأجل مُسمى ...
فلتصبر ولتحتسب "



وإن قال :
"أعظم الله أجرك ،
وأحسن عزاءك
وغفر لميتك"
فحسن
items.transliteration: Inna lillāhi mā akhadh, wa lahu mā a`tā, wa kullu shay'in `indahu bi ajalin musammā . . . faltaṣbir wa ‘l-taḥtasib . [Also good to say: ] A`ẓamallāhu ajrak, wa 'aḥsana `azā'ak, wa ghafara limayyitik.
items.english:
Surely, Allah takes what is His,
and what He gives is His,
and to all things He has appointed a time...
so have patience and be rewarded.1

May Allah magnify your reward,
and make perfect your bereavement,
and forgive your departed.2
items.reference: 1 Al-Bukhari 2/80, Muslim 2/636. 2An-Nawawi, Kitabul-'Athkar, p. 126. Hisn al-Muslim 162

cat58.title: Placing the deceased in the grave / دعاء عند إدخال الميت القبر

items.arabic:
بِسْـمِ اللهِ
وَعَلـى سُـنَّةِ رَسـولِ الله
items.transliteration: Bismillāh wa `alā sunnati Rasūlillāh.
items.english:
With the Name of Allah,
and according to the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 3/314 with an authentic chain. Ahmad also recorded it with the wording: With the Name of Allah, and according to the religion of the Messenger of Allah. Its chain is also authentic. Hisn al-Muslim 163

cat59.title: After burying the deceased / دعاء بعد دفن الميت

items.arabic:
اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ
اللَّهُمَّ ثَبِّتْهُ
items.transliteration: Allāhumma’ghfir lah Allāhumma thabbit’h.
items.english:
O Allah, forgive him.
O Allah, strengthen him.
items.reference: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to stop after burying the dead and say to the people: "Ask Allah to forgive your brother and pray for him to be strengthened, for indeed he is now being questioned." Abu Dawud 3/315, and Al-Hakim 1/370 who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed. Hisn al-Muslim 164

cat60.title: Visiting the graves / دعاء زيارة القبور

items.arabic:
السَّلامُ عَلَـيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الدِّيارِ مِنَ المؤْمِنيـنَ وَالْمُسْلِمين،
وَإِنّا إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ بِكُـمْ لاحِقـون،
نَسْـاَلُ اللهَ لنـا وَلَكُـمْ العـافِيَة
items.transliteration: Assalāmu `alaykum ahlad-diyāri minal-mu'minīna wa ‘l-muslimīn, wa innā in shā' Allāhu bikum lāḥiqūn nas'alullāha lanā wa lakumul-`āfiyah.
items.english:
Peace be upon you, people of this abode, from among the believers and those who are Muslims,
and we, by the Will of Allah, shall be joining you.
[May Allah have mercy on the first of us and the last of us]
I ask Allah to grant us and you well-being.
items.reference: Muslim 2/671, Ibn Majah 1/494, the portion brackets is from Muslim 2/671. Hisn al-Muslim 165

cat61.title: During a wind storm / دعاء الريح

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِنَّـي أَسْـأَلُـكَ خَيْـرَها،
وَأَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَـرِّها
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innī as'aluka khayrahā, wa a`ūdhu bika min sharrihā.
items.english:
O Allah, I ask You for the good of it,
and seek refuge in You against its evil.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 4/326, Ibn Majah 2/1228. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 2/305. Hisn al-Muslim 166

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِنَّـي أَسْـأَلُـكَ خَيْـرَها،
وَخَيْـرَ ما فيهـا،
وَخَيْـرَ ما اُرْسِلَـتْ بِه،
وَأَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَـرِّها،
وَشَـرِّ ما فيهـا،
وَشَـرِّ ما اُرْسِلَـتْ بِه
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innī as'aluka khayrahā, wa khayra mā fīhā, wa khayra mā ursilat bih, wa a`ūdhu bika min sharrihā, wa sharri mā fīhā, wa sharri mā ursilat bih.
items.reference: Muslim 2/616, Al-Bukhari 4/76. Hisn al-Muslim 167

items.arabic:
سُبْـحانَ الّذي يُسَبِّـحُ الـرَّعْدُ بِحَمْـدِهِ،
وَالملائِكـةُ مِنْ خيـفَته
items.transliteration: Subḥāna ‘l-ladhī yusabbiḥur-ra`du bi ḥamdihi wa ‘l-malā'ikatu min khīfatih.
items.english: Glory is to Him Whom thunder and angels glorify due to fear of Him.
items.reference: Whenever Abdullah bin Zubair (RA) would hear thunder, he would abandon all conversation and say this supplication. See Al-Muwatta' 2/992. It was graded authentic by Al-Albani as a statement of Abdullah bin Zubayr only. Hisn al-Muslim 168

items.arabic:
اللّهُمَّ اسْقِـنا غَيْـثاً مُغيـثاً مَريئاً مُريـعاً،
نافِعـاً غَيْـرَ ضار،
عاجِـلاً غَـيْرَ آجِل
items.transliteration: Allāhumma ‘asqinā ghaythan mughīthan marī'an murī`a, nāfi`an ghayra ḍārr, `ājilan ghayra ājil.
items.english: O Allah, shower upon us abundant rain, beneficial not harmful, swiftly and not delayed.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 1/303. See also Al-Albani SahihAbu Dawud 1/216. Hisn al-Muslim 169

items.arabic:
اللّهُمَّ أغِثْنـا،
اللّهُمَّ أغِثْنـا،
اللّهُمَّ أغِثْنـا
items.transliteration: Allāhumma aghithnā, Allāhumma aghithnā, Allāhumma aghithnā.
items.english:
O Allah, send us rain.
O Allah, send us rain.
O Allah, send us rain.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 1/224, Muslim 2/613. Hisn al-Muslim 170

items.arabic:
اللّهُمَّ اسْقِ عِبادَكَ وَبَهـائِمَك،
وَانْشُـرْ رَحْمَـتَكَ
وَأَحْيِي بَلَـدَكَ المَيِّـت
items.transliteration: Allāhumma’sqi `ibādaka wa bahā'imak, wanshur raḥmatak, wa aḥyi baladaka ‘l-mayyit
items.english:
O Allah, give water to Your slaves, and Your livestock,
and spread Your mercy,
and revive Your dead land.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 1/305. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Abu Dawud 1/218. Hisn al-Muslim 171

cat64.title: When it rains / الدعاء إذا رأى المطر

items.arabic: اللّهُمَّ صَيِّـباً نافِـعاً
items.transliteration: Allāhumma ṣayyiban nāfi`a.
items.english: O Allah, (bring) beneficial rain clouds.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 2/518. Hisn al-Muslim 172

cat65.title: After rainfall / الذكر بعد نزول المطر

items.arabic: مُطِـرْنا بِفَضْـلِ اللهِ وَرَحْمَـتِه
items.transliteration: Muṭirnā bifaḍlillāhi wa raḥmatih.
items.english: We have been given rain by the grace and mercy of Allah.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 1/205, Muslim 1/83. Hisn al-Muslim 173

cat66.title: Asking for clear skies / من أدعية الاستصحاء

items.arabic:
اللّهُمَّ حَوالَيْنا وَلا عَلَيْـنا،
اللّهُمَّ عَلى الآكـامِ وَالظِّـراب،
وَبُطـونِ الأوْدِية، وَمَنـابِتِ الشَّجـر
items.transliteration: Allāhumma ḥawālaynā wa lā `alaynā. Allāhumma `ala ‘l-ākāmi waẓ-ẓirāb, wa buṭūni ‘l-awdiyati, wa manābitish-shajar.
items.english:
O Allah, let the rain fall around us and not upon us,
O Allah, (let it fall) on the pastures, hills, valleys, and the roots of trees.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 1/224, Muslim 1/614. Hisn al-Muslim 174

cat67.title: Upon sighting the crescent moon / دعاء رؤية الهلال

items.arabic:
اللهُ أَكْـبَر،
اللّهُمَّ أَهِلَّـهُ عَلَيْـنا بِالأمْـنِ وَالإيمـان،
والسَّلامَـةِ والإسْلام،
وَالتَّـوْفيـقِ لِما تُحِـبُّ رَبَّنـا وَتَـرْضـى،
رَبُّنـا وَرَبُّكَ الله
items.transliteration: Allāhu Akbar, Allāhumma ahillahu `alayna bi ‘l-amni wa ‘l-īmān, was-salāmati wa ‘l-'Islām, wat-tawfīqi limā tuḥibbu Rabbanā wa tarḍā, Rabbunā wa Rabbukallāh.
items.english:
Allah is the Most Great.
O Allah, bring us the new moon with security and Faith,
with peace and in Islam,
and in harmony with what our Lord loves and what pleases Him.
Our Lord and your Lord is Allah.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/504, Ad-Darimi 1/336. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/157. Hisn al-Muslim 175

cat68.title: Upon breaking fast / الدعاء عند إفطار الصائم

items.arabic:
ذَهَـبَ الظَّمَـأُ،
وَابْتَلَّـتِ العُـروق،
وَثَبَـتَ الأجْـرُ إِنْ شـاءَ الله
items.transliteration: Dhahabaẓ-ẓama', wabtallati ‘l-`urūq, wa thabata ‘l-'ajru in shā Allāh.
items.english:
The thirst is gone,
the veins are moistened,
and the reward is confirmed, if Allah wills.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 2/306 and others. See also Al- Albani, Sahihul-Jami' As-Saghir 4/209. Hisn al-Muslim 176

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ إِنَّـي أَسْـأَلُـكَ بِرَحْمَـتِكَ الّتي وَسِـعَت كُلَّ شيء،
أَنْ تَغْـفِرَ لي
items.transliteration: Allāhumma innī as'aluka bi raḥmatika ‘l-latī wasi`at kulla shay' an taghfira lī.
items.english: O Allah, I ask You by Your mercy, which encompasses all things, that You forgive me.
items.reference: Ibn Majah 1/557 from a supplication of Abdullah bin 'Amr. Al-Hafidh graded it as good in his checking of An-Nawawi's Kitabul-'Athkdr. See Sharhul- Athkar 4/342. Hisn al-Muslim 177

items.arabic:
إِذَا أَكَلَ أَحَدُكُمْ طَعَاماً فَلْيَقُلْ
بِسْمِ اللَّهِ،
فَإِنْ نَسِيَ فِي أَوَّلِهِ فَلْيَقُلْ
بسمِ اللَّهِ فِي أَوَّلِهِ وَآخِرِهِ
items.transliteration: When anyone of you begins eating, say: Bismillāh. And if you forget then when you remember, say: Bismillāhi fī awwalihi wa ākhirih.
items.english:
When anyone of you begins eating, say:

With the Name of Allah.

And if you forget then, when you remember, say:

With the Name of Allah, in the beginning, and in the end.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 3/347, At-Tirmidhi 4/288. See Al- Albani's Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/167. Hisn al-Muslim 178

items.arabic:
مَنْ أَطْعَمَهُ اللَّهُ الطَّعَامَ فَلْيَقُلْ:
"اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيهِ
وَأَطْعِمْنَا خَيْراً مِنْهُ"،

وَمَنْ سَقَاهُ اللَّهُ لَبَناً فَلْيَقُلْ
"اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيهِ
وَزِدْنَا مِنْهُ"
items.transliteration: Whomever Allah has given food, should say: Allāhumma bārik lanā fīhi, wa aṭ`imnā khayran minh. Whomever Allah has given milk to drink, should say : Allāhumma bārik lanā fīhi, wa zidnā minh.
items.english:
Whomever Allah has given food, should say:

O Allah, bless us in it and provide us with better than it.

Whomever Allah has given milk to drink, should say :

O Allah, bless us in it and give us more of it.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/506. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/158. Hisn al-Muslim 179

cat70.title: Upon completing the meal / الدعاء عند الفراغ من الطعام

items.arabic:
الْحَمْـدُ للهِ الَّذي أَطْعَمَنـي هـذا
وَرَزَقَنـيهِ مِنْ غَـيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلا قُوَّة
items.transliteration: Alhamdu lillāhi ‘l-ladhī aṭ`amanī hādhā, wa razaqanīhi min ghayri ḥawlin minnī wa lā quwwah.
items.english:
Praise is to Allah Who has given me this food,
and sustained me with it though I was unable to do it and powerless.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud, and Ibn Majah. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/159. Hisn al-Muslim 180

items.arabic:
الْحَمْـدُ للهِ حَمْـداً كَثـيراً طَيِّـباً مُبـارَكاً فيه،
غَيْرَ مَكْفِيٍّ وَلا مُوَدَّعٍ وَلا مُسْتَغْـنىً عَنْـهُ رَبَّـنا
items.transliteration: Alhamdu lillāhi ḥamdan kathīran tayyiban mubārakan fīh, ghayra makfiyyin wa lā muwadda`in, wa lā mustaghnan `anhu Rabbanā.
items.english: All praise is to Allah, praise in abundance, good and blessed. It cannot [be compensated for, nor can it] be left, nor can it be done without, our Lord.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 6/214, At-Tirmidhi 5/507. Hisn al-Muslim 181

cat71.title: Of the guest for the host / دعاء الضيف لصاحب الطعام

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ بارِكْ لَهُمْ فيما رَزَقْـتَهُم،
وَاغْفِـرْ لَهُـمْ وَارْحَمْهُمْ
items.transliteration: Allāhumma bārik lahum fī mā razaqtahum, wagh’fir lahum warḥamhum.
items.english:
O Allah, bless them in what You have provided for them,
and forgive them and have mercy on them.
items.reference: Muslim 3/1615. Hisn al-Muslim 182

cat72.title: To one who intends to give food or drink / الدعاء إذا أراد الطعام أو الشراب

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ أَطْعِمْ مَن أَطْعَمَني،
وَاسْقِ مَن سقاني
items.transliteration: Allāhumma aṭ`im man aṭ`amanī wasqi man saqānī.
items.english:
O Allah, feed the one who has fed me,
and give drink to the one who has given me drink.
items.reference: Muslim 3/126. Hisn al-Muslim 183

cat73.title: When breaking fast in someone’s home / الدعاء إذا أفطر عند أهل بيت

items.arabic:
أَفْطَـرَ عِنْدَكُم الصّـائِمونَ
وَأَكَلَ طَعامَـكُمُ الأبْـرار،
وَصَلَّـتْ عَلَـيْكُمُ الملائِكَـة
items.transliteration: Afṭara `indakumuṣ-ṣā'imūn, wa akala ṭa`āmakumu ‘l-'abrār, wa ṣallat `alaykumu ‘l-malā'ikah.
items.english:
With you, those who are fasting have broken their fast,
you have fed those who are righteous,
and the angels recite their prayers upon you.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 3/367, Ibn Majah 1/556, An-Nasa'i, 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah 296-8. Al-Albani graded it authentic in Sahih Abu Dawud 2/730. Hisn al-Muslim 184

cat74.title: By one fasting when presented with food and does not break his fast / دعاء الصائم إذا حضر الطعام ولم يفطر

items.arabic: (إِذَا دُعِيَ أَحَدُكُمْ فَلْيُجِبْ، فَإِنْ كَانَ صَائِماً فَلْيُصَلِّ، وَإِنْ كَانَ مُفْطِراً فَلْيَطْعَمْ) ، وَمَعْنَى فَلْيُصَلِّ أَيْ فَلْيَدْعُ.
items.english:
When you are invited (to eat) then reply to the invitation.
If you are fasting then invoke Allah's blessings (on your host),
and if you are not fasting then eat.
items.reference: Muslim 2/1054. Hisn al-Muslim 185

cat75.title: When insulted while fasting / ما يقول الصائم إذا سابه أحد

items.arabic:
إِنِّي صَائِمٌ،
إِنِّي صَائِمٌ
items.transliteration: Innī ṣā'im, innī ṣā'im.
items.english:
I am fasting.
I am fasting.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-BAri 4/103, Muslim 2/806. Hisn al-Muslim 186

cat76.title: Upon seeing the early or premature fruit / الدعاء عند رؤية باكورة الثمر

items.arabic:
اللّهُـمَّ بارِكْ لَنا في ثَمَـرِنا،
وَبارِكْ لَنا في مَدينَتِنـا،
وَبارِكْ لَنا في صاعِنـا،
وَبارِكْ لَنا في مُدِّنا
items.transliteration: Allahumma bārik lanā fī thamarinā, wa bārik lanā fī madīnatinā wa bārik lanā fī ṣā`inā, wa bārik lanā fī muddinā.
items.english:
O Allah, bless us in our dates and bless us in our town,
bless us in our Sa' and in our Mudd.
items.reference: Muslim 2/1000 (Sa' and Mudd are both dry measures used for agricultural produce by the Arabs in the Prophet's time. Of the two, the So.' was the larger measure.) (Translator) Hisn al-Muslim 187

items.arabic:
إِذَا عَطَسَ أَحَدُكُم فَلْيَقُلِ (الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ)،
وَلْيَقُلْ لَهُ أَخُوهُ أَوْ صَاحِبُهُ: (يَرْحَمُكَ اللَّهُ)،

فَإِذَا قَالَ لَهُ: (يَرحَمُكَ اللَّهُ)، فَلْيَقُلْ: (يَهْدِيكُمُ اللَّهُ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَكُمْ)
items.transliteration: When you sneeze , then say: Alḥamdulillāh Your companion should say : Yarḥamukallāh When someone says Yarḥamukallāh to you then you should say: Yahdīkumu ‘llāhu wa yuṣliḥu bālakum.
items.english:
When you sneeze, then say:
All praises and thanks are to Allah.

Your companion should say:
May Allah have mercy upon you.

When someone says Yarḥamukallāh to you then you should say:
May Allah guide you and set your affairs in order.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 7/125. Hisn al-Muslim 188

cat78.title: When a disbeliever praises Allah after sneezing / ما يقالُ للكافر إذا عطس فحمد الله

items.arabic: يَهْـديكُـمُ اللهُ وَيُصْـلِحُ بالَـكُم
items.transliteration: Yahdīkumullāhu wa yuṣliḥu bālakum.
items.english: May Allah guide you and set your affairs in order
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/82, Ahmad 4/400, Abu Dawud 4/ 308. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/354. Hisn al-Muslim 189

items.arabic:
بارَكَ اللّهُ لَك،
وَبارَكَ عَلَـيْك،
وَجَمَعَ بَيْـنَكُما في خَـيْر
items.transliteration: Bārakallāhu lak, wa bāraka `alayk, wa jama`a baynakumā fī khayr.
items.english:
May Allah bless you,
and shower His blessings upon you,
and join you together in goodness.
items.reference: Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah and At-Tirmidhi. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 1/316. Hisn al-Muslim 190

cat80.title: On the wedding night or when buying an animal / دعاء المتزوج لنفسه ودعاء شراء الدابة

items.arabic:
إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ أَحَدُكُمُ امْرَأَةً، أَوْ إِذَا اشْتَرَى خَادِماً فَلْيَقُلْ:
(اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَهَا،
وَخَيْرَ مَا جَبَلْتَهَا عَلَيْهِ،
وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا،
وَشَرِّ مَا جَبَلْتَهَا عَلَيْهِ)،
وَإِذَا اشْتَرَى بَعِيراً فَلْيَأْخُذْ بِذِرْوَةِ سَنَامِهِ وَلْيَقُلْ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ.
items.transliteration: When any of you marries a woman or purchases a maid-servant then let him say: Allāhumma innī as'aluka khayrahā wa khayra mā jabaltahā `alayh, wa a`ūdhu bika min sharrihā wa sharri mā jabaltahā `alayh.
items.english:
When any of you marries a woman or purchases a maid-servant then let him say :

O Allah, I ask You for the goodness of her,
and the goodness upon which You have created her,
and I seek refuge in You from the evil of her,
and from the evil upon which You have created her.

If you purchase a camel then take hold of the top of its hump and say the same.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 2/248 and Ibn Majah 1/617. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Ibn Majah 1/324. Hisn al-Muslim 191

cat81.title: Before sexual intercourse with the wife / الدعاء قبل إتيان الزوجة

items.arabic:
بِسْمِ الله
اللّهُـمَّ جَنِّبْنا الشَّيْـطانَ،
وَجَنِّبِ الشَّـيْطانَ ما رَزَقْـتَنا
items.transliteration: Bismillāh. Allāhumma jannibnash-Shayṭān, wa jannibish-Shayṭāna mā razaqtanā.
items.english:
With the Name of Allah.
O Allah, keep the Devil away from us,
and keep the Devil away from that which You provide for us.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 6/141, Muslim 2/1028. Hisn al-Muslim 192

items.arabic: أَعـوذُ بِاللهِ مِنَ الشَّيْـطانِ الرَّجيـم
items.transliteration: A`ūdhu billāhi minash-Shayṭānir-rajīm.
items.english: I seek refuge in Allah from Satan the outcast.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari 7/99, Muslim 4/2015. Hisn al-Muslim 193

cat83.title: Upon seeing someone in trial or tribulation / دعاء من رأى مبتلى

items.arabic:
الْحَمْـدُ للهِ الّذي عافاني مِمّا ابْتَـلاكَ بِهِ،
وَفَضَّلَـني عَلى كَثيـرٍ مِمَّنْ خَلَـقَ تَفْضـيلا
items.transliteration: Alhamdu lillāhi ‘l-ladhī `āfānī mimmab-talāka bihi wa faḍḍalanī `alā kathīrin mimman khalaqa tafḍīlā.
items.english:
Praise is to Allah Who has spared me what He has afflicted you with,
and preferred me greatly above much of what He has created.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi 5/493,4. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/153. Hisn al-Muslim 194

cat84.title: At a sitting or gathering / ما يقال في المجلس

items.arabic:
عن ابن عمر قال : كان يعد لرسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم في المجلس الواحد مائة مرة من قبل أن يقوم
"رب اغفر لي
وتب علي
إنك أنت التواب الغفور"
items.transliteration: Ibn Umar (ra) said: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to repeat in a single sitting: Rabbigh’fir lī wa tub `alayy innaka antat-Tawwābu ‘l-Ghafūr.
items.english:
Ibn Umar (ra) said: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) used to repeat in a single sitting:

My Lord, forgive me,
and accept my repentance,
You are the Ever-Relenting, the All-Forgiving.
items.reference: Sahih Ibn Majah 2/321. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/153. Hisn al-Muslim 195

cat85.title: For the expiation of sins, said at the conclusion of a sitting or gathering / كفارة المجلس

items.arabic:
سُبْحـانَكَ اللّهُـمَّ وَبِحَمدِك،
أَشْهَـدُ أَنْ لا إِلهَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ
أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتوبُ إِلَـيْك
items.transliteration: Subhānaka Allāhumma wa biḥamdik, ash-hadu an lā ilāha illā ant, astaghfiruka wa atību ilayk.
items.english:
Glory is to You, O Allah, and praise is to You.
I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship but You.
I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You.
items.reference: Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, At-Tirmidhi and An-Nasa'i. See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 3/ 153. Aishah (RA) said: "Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) did not sit in a gathering, and did not recite the Qur'an, and did not perform any prayer without concluding by saying ... (then she quoted the above)." This was reported by An-Nasa'i in 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah (no.308), and Dr. Farooq Hamadah graded it authentic in his checking of the same book, p. 273. See also Ahmad 6/77. Hisn al-Muslim 196

cat86.title: Returning a supplication of forgiveness / الدعاء لمن قال غفر الله لك

items.arabic: وَلَكَ
items.transliteration: Walaka
items.english: And you.
items.reference: Ahmad 5/82, and An-Nasa'i in 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah p. 218, with checking by Dr. Farooq Hamadah. Hisn al-Muslim 197

cat87.title: To one who does you a favour / الدعاء لمن صنع إليك معروفاً

items.arabic: جَزاكَ اللهُ خَـيْراً
items.transliteration: Jazākallāhu khayra.
items.english: May Allah reward you with good.
items.reference: At-Tirmidhi (no. 2035). See also Al-Albani, Sahih At-Tirmidhi 2/200 and Sahihul-Jami' (no. 6244). Hisn al-Muslim 198

cat88.title: Protection from the Dajjal / الذكر الذي يعصم الله به من الدجال

items.arabic: .(مَنْ حَفِظَ عَشْرَ آيَاتٍ مِنْ أَوَّلِ سُورَةِ الْكَهْفِ عُصِمَ مِنَ الدَّجَّالِ) ، وَالْاسْتِعَاذَةُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ فِتْنَتِهِ عَقِبَ التَّشَهُّدِ الْأَخِيرِ مِنْ كُلِّ صَلاَةٍ.
items.english:
Whoever memorizes ten 'Ayat (Verses) from the beginning of Surat Al-Kahf, will be protected from the False Messiah 1
if he recites in every prayer after the final Tashahhud before ending the prayer, seeking the protection of Allah from the trials of the False Messiah.
items.reference: 1 Muslim 1/555, another version mentions the last ten 'Ayat, Muslim 1/556. 2 See invocations no. 55 and 56 of this book. Hisn al-Muslim 199

cat89.title: To one who pronounces his love for you, for Allah’s sake / الدعاء لمن قال إني أحبك في الله

items.arabic: أَحَبَّـكَ الّذي أَحْبَبْـتَني لَه
items.transliteration: Aḥabbaka ‘lladhī aḥbabtanī lah.
items.english: May He for Whose sake you love me, love you.
items.reference: Abu Dawud 4/333. Al-Albani graded it good in Sahih Abu Dawud 3/965. Hisn al-Muslim 200

cat90.title: To one who has offered you some of his wealth / الدعاء لمن عرض عليك ماله

items.arabic: بارَكَ اللهُ لَكَ في أَهْلِكَ وَمالِك
items.transliteration: Bārakallāhu laka fī ahlika wa mālik.
items.english: May Allah bless you in your family and your property.
items.reference: Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-BAri 4/88. Hisn al-Muslim 201
